prompt:
I run a story-night group and we design little text adventures on index cards; I want a proper engine so the cards become playable. Java, stdlib only. Please structure it as a real OO model — I want typed Room, Item and Player classes I can poke at from other tools, a World that parses our card format, and an Engine that plays a session. (Room.java, Item.java, Player.java, World.java, Engine.java is the shape I'd expect.)

Our world files (worlds/*.world) are plain text, one statement per line; blank lines and '#' comments are skipped. Statements: 'room <id> <Title words...>', 'desc <id> <one line of text>', 'exit <room> <north|south|east|west> <dest>', 'door <room> <dir> <dest> <key-item>' (a locked one-way passage), 'item <room> <item-id>', 'start <room>', 'goal <room>'. Order in the file must not matter — rooms may be referenced before they're declared. World.parse(String) validates everything with IllegalArgumentException: unknown statement kinds, malformed lines, bad directions, references to undeclared rooms, duplicate room ids, duplicate desc, two passages out of one room in the same direction, item ids used twice, door keys that exist nowhere, missing/duplicate start or goal, and any room left without a desc.

Typed surface I need: World.rooms() (map id -> Room), World.room(id). Room: id(), title(), description(), items() (typed Items, sorted by name), exitDirections() in fixed compass order north, south, east, west. Item.name(). Engine(world) starts a session; engine.player() gives the Player with location() and inventory() (sorted). engine.finished().

engine.exec(command) plays one command and returns the story's reply, exactly: 'look' gives the room block — title line, desc line, 'You see: a, b.' (sorted, line omitted when empty), 'Exits: north, east (locked).' (compass order, ' (locked)' on locked doors, 'Exits: none.' when sealed). 'go <dir>' answers 'You can't go <dir>.' / 'The door to the <dir> is locked.' or moves and returns the new room's block. 'take <x>' / 'drop <x>' answer 'Taken.' / 'Dropped.' / 'There is no <x> here.' / 'You don't have <x>.'. 'inventory' answers 'You carry nothing.' / 'You carry: a, b.'. 'unlock <dir>' answers 'There is no locked door to the <dir>.' (also for already-open or plain exits), 'You need <key>.' when the key isn't carried, else 'You unlock the door with <key>.' — the door then stays open, the key stays with you. Anything else (wrong arity included): 'I don't understand.'. Walking into the goal room appends '*** You made it! ***' under the room block and finishes the story; from then on every command answers 'The story is over.'.

SAVE/LOAD, the part I really need for club nights: engine.save() returns one state string, exactly 'loc=<room>;inv=<items,comma,sorted>;open=<room/dir,comma,sorted>;rooms=<id>:<items+plus+sorted>,...' with rooms sorted by id and every room listed (empty sections stay as bare 'inv=' etc.); saving a finished story is IllegalStateException. Engine.restore(world, state) resumes: it must round-trip (restore(w, s).save() equals s), keep opened doors open, and reject with IllegalArgumentException any malformed string, unknown room/item, opened entry with no door there, an item appearing twice, or a world item missing entirely — a save may never duplicate or lose an object.

Two of our worlds ship as fixtures (worlds/manor.world, worlds/depot.world) and TestMain.java replays them end to end — 'java TestMain.java' is the acceptance gate. Tests and world files are read-only.
notes: 7G6 item 1: OO text-adventure engine with typed Room/Item/Player model and pinned SAVE/LOAD state string. Differentiation vs py-adventure (same wave, 7G1): typed class surface poked directly by tests + save/restore round-trip with item-conservation validation vs py's JSON-data-driven world; vs wave-4 ts-text-adventure: data-file worlds + save strings vs its code-defined world. All exec replies and both save strings hand-written before the reference existed; reference passed 26/26 first run. Pins: parse validation matrix (12 cases), compass-order exits with (locked) tags, look/go/take/drop/inventory/unlock reply grammar, goal detection + story-over lockout, save format, restore round-trip incl. opened doors, restore rejection of duplicated/lost items and doorless open entries, two worlds on one engine.
